programme.title: Diving
programme.desc: From Windsor, Ontario.

programme.title: Live: CBC Ontario News
programme.desc: A comprehensive look at the day's top stories.

programme.title: Still Standing
programme.desc: The seafaring town of Gibson, British Columbia, once made famous for "The Beachcombers" is reinventing itself as a regional hub for Filipinos.

programme.title: Rock League
programme.desc: Championship Final.

programme.title: Summit '72
programme.desc: They are called the greatest team ever assembled, but Team Canada find themselves outmatched by a disciplined Soviet team.

programme.title: Summit '72
programme.desc: The Soviets show their victory in Game 1 was no accident, and Canadian fans show their frustrations as Team Canada struggles to keep up with Soviet-style hockey.

programme.title: Summit '72
programme.sub-title: Finding Each Other Behind the Iron Curtain
programme.desc: Team Canada and a legion of loyal fans travel into hostile territory behind the Iron Curtain for the final four games of the series.

programme.title: Summit '72
programme.desc: Team Canada confronts the challenge of a lifetime in the final game of the Summit Series.

programme.title: Rivals: The 4 Nations Face-Off
programme.desc: With Canadian national identity on the line, longstanding hockey rivals fight to the finish.

programme.title: This Hour Has 22 Minutes
programme.desc: Navigating the travel rules, as the government clamps down; actor William Shatner; actress Mary Walsh; actor Patrick Roach.

programme.title: This Hour Has 22 Minutes
programme.desc: Looking for love with Valentine's Day just around the corner; "Undercover Boss" comes to Rideau Hall; Mrs. Enid returns.

programme.title: This Hour Has 22 Minutes
programme.desc: The world continues to adapt to the pandemic; provincial economies prepare to reopen; impeachment enters round two.

programme.title: This Hour Has 22 Minutes
programme.desc: What's happening at home; new search for a governor-general; truckers take on a Hank Snow parody.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple hope their £400,000 budget will buy them a slice of rural bliss in Scotland.

programme.title: Gary's Magic Fort
programme.desc: When Gary hosts a tea party, he brings everything except his manners; after a rough start to the party, Gary's friends help him learn what manners are, and how to use them.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri sings a song about how wonderful it is to have your very own body.

programme.desc: Daisy sees some interesting bugs in her garden; Daisy and the Gumboot Kids gather some supplies and craft some cute critters.

programme.desc: The Gumboot Kids visit a bison farm; Jessie Farrell sings about bison, and Scout and Daisy join in on the fun.

programme.title: Scout & the Gumboot Kids
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Happy Hummer
programme.desc: Scout sends the Gumboot Kids to a ranch to find the happy hummer.

programme.title: Mittens & Pants
programme.desc: Mittens and Pants try to figure out the words to DJ Swimfins' song, "Icky Icky Wah Wah"; no matter who they ask, everyone has a different idea what the lyrics are.

programme.desc: Dylan and his friends imagine he's a mail carrier.

programme.desc: Benny and Penny get stuck together just before Penny's dance-off.

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Driscolls make a shocking discovery as Megan's lie is exposed.

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel seeks answers from Megan about the future of their relationship.

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo ensures that Todd misses his marathon; Will refuses to join his family in condemning Megan; Adam suffers a loss of business; Bernie and Dev find Mal waiting upon their return from vacation.

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo tries a charm offensive on George and Christina; Kit panics when Mal names his attacker; Betsy and Dylan babysit Connie; Hope warns Sam against listening to Daniel.

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo stuns Todd by suggesting they get married right away, Bernie experiences divided loyalties to her son and husband, and Will struggles with being the focus of local gossip.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang explains how Prime Minister Mark Carney was able to achieve a majority government without a general election; plus, why Alberta's separation from Canada is almost impossible.

programme.title: Rosemary Barton Live
programme.desc: Rosemary Barton delivers a weekly dose of compelling conversations and need-to-know political news and issues affecting Canadians.

programme.title: Marketplace
programme.desc: After investigating customer concerns about AGM Renovations, the company abruptly shuts down; Marketplace speaks to customers left in the lurch and travels overseas, searching for the man behind the operation.

programme.title: Land and Sea
programme.desc: Keeping rare purebred poultry thriving; we'll follow Nova Scotians to the biggest purebred poultry show in Canada.

programme.title: Still Standing
programme.desc: Temagami, Ontario, is synonymous with old-growth and the struggle to balance economics and conservation.

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours in...
programme.desc: Nish Kumar joins Richard for a trip around Porto, Portugal; their stuffed mini-break includes scooters, sandwiches, pastries, confession, and some memorable views of Portugal's second city.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Sal and Fiona's biggest challenge is agreeing on a location and design of their new home; the solution: Sal decides the location and Fiona decides the design - a contemporary home, made from concrete.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Tim converts a 180-year-old wine barrel into a quirky dining space; Sasha creates a picture-perfect Mediterranean garden; Andrea and David view a property formally inhabited by one of The Three Musketeers.

programme.title: The Great Canadian Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: The potters create chess sets and, in a throw down speed challenge, make tulips cups.

programme.title: Just for Laughs: Gags
programme.desc: There's no sound, but there sure are plenty of laughs. This crazy Quebec-based troupe uses the city as its stage, and its inhabitants, or victims, as characters! People are caught in a twisted yet funny web of comedic deception. This updated "Candid Camera" is a tad more risque and a little kookier with its practical jokes. The little snippets last only a few minutes, and some look more painful than others.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação
Dirección General de Radio, Televisión y Cinematografía

programme.title: Live: NHL Hockey
programme.desc: Game 1 of the Eastern Conference First Round between the Montreal Canadiens and the Tampa Bay Lightning takes place at Benchmark International Arena. The regular-season series was closely contested, with both teams splitting their four meetings 2-2.

programme.title: The Great Canadian Baking Show
programme.desc: Ten amateur bakers from Canada arrive at the Baking Tent to start their quest for the cake plate.

programme.title: Crime Scene Kitchen
programme.desc: Six pairs of family members put their culinary forensic skills to the test by putting together clues from the kitchen to recreate desserts that were baked there.

programme.title: Live: NHL Hockey
programme.desc: The Utah Mammoth and the Vegas Golden Knights meet for Game 1 of the Western Conference First Round at T-Mobile Arena. Utah won the regular-season series 2-1, including a dominant 4-0 shutout on March 19. Jack Eichel leads Vegas with 90 points.
